en:
Last autumn, villagers in a quiet valley reported a large cat near the woods.
At first, experts paid little attention to these strange stories.
Within a month, however, the reports had accumulated, and huge paw prints were found by a stream.
The prints were far too large, so the animal can't have been an ordinary pet.
Then a sheep was killed, and deep, disturbing scratches were left on several trees.
Experts finally agreed that the cat must have escaped from a private collection.
The hunt went on for weeks, but the mysterious animal was never caught.
cn:
去年秋天，一个宁静山谷里的村民报告说树林附近出现了一只大型猫科动物。
起初，专家们对这些离奇的说法并不在意。
然而不到一个月，报告越积越多，溪边还发现了巨大的爪印。
爪印实在太大，这只动物不可能是普通的宠物。
接着一只羊被咬死，几棵树上留下了很深的、令人不安的抓痕。
专家们最终一致认为，这只大猫一定是从某个私人收藏者那里逃出来的。
搜捕持续了数周，但这只神秘的动物始终没有落网。

en:
Our vicar has been raising money to repair the church clock for two years.
Last night, strange sounds came from the bell tower at midnight.
Armed with a torch, the vicar climbed the dark stairs to see what was happening.
There he found old Bill, who has been trying to fix the bell night after night.
"You have certainly given everyone a surprise," the vicar said with a smile.
The bell has started working again, but it strikes thirteen times at one o'clock.
The villagers have already got used to this funny little mistake.
cn:
两年来，我们的牧师一直在为修理教堂的钟筹款。
昨天午夜，钟楼里传出了奇怪的声音。
牧师拿着手电筒，爬上漆黑的楼梯去看个究竟。
他在那里发现了老比尔——比尔一晚接一晚地一直在设法修钟。
“你可真是给大家一个惊喜。”牧师笑着说。
钟又开始报时了，只是一点钟时会敲十三下。
村民们已经习惯了这个有趣的小毛病。

en:
Last year, a team of archaeologists explored a rocky hill close to the Aegean Sea.
They soon proved that a prosperous city had stood there thousands of years ago.
Houses, streets and even a simple drainage system were uncovered one by one.
In a small sacred room, fragments of a stone figure were found under the dust.
The pieces have been carefully reconstructed into the figure of a graceful goddess.
Clearly, she had been worshipped by the people of this early civilization.
Her identity, however, remains a mystery, for no name was carved on the stone.
cn:
去年，一支考古队勘察了爱琴海附近的一座石山。
他们很快证明，几千年前这里曾矗立着一座繁荣的城市。
房屋、街道，甚至一套简单的排水系统被一一发掘出来。
在一间小小的圣室里，人们从尘土下发现了一尊石像的碎片。
这些碎片已被小心地复原成一位体态优雅的女神像。
显然，她曾受到这个早期文明的人们的崇拜。
然而她的身份仍是个谜，因为石头上没有刻下任何名字。

en:
My neighbour Frank has worked for the Corporation as a dustman for ten years.
He is a man who takes pride in his work, but he has always kept it secret.
Every morning he leaves home in a smart suit, which makes people think he works in an office.
At the depot he changes into overalls, and after work he has a shower and changes back.
He has never told the neighbours the truth, because he worries about his status.
His wife, who has known the secret from the start, thinks he has sacrificed too much.
In her eyes, honest manual work is nothing to be ashamed of.
cn:
我的邻居弗兰克在市政部门当清洁工已经十年了。
他为自己的工作感到自豪，却一直对此保密。
每天早上他都穿着笔挺的西装出门，让人们以为他在办公室上班。
到了车场他换上工作服，下班后冲个澡再换回西装。
他从未把真相告诉邻居，因为他在意自己的身份地位。
他的妻子从一开始就知道这个秘密，她觉得他牺牲得太多了。
在她看来，诚实的体力劳动没有什么可羞愧的。

en:
A magazine editor once planned to publish an article about a president's new palace.
To get the facts, he sent a young journalist to that distant country.
The journalist wanted to describe everything, so he wrote more than a hundred pages.
When the editor read the article, he found that the writer had left out one small figure.
He sent an urgent fax to ask how much the palace had cost.
The impatient editor waited for days, but no answer came back.
In the end, the journalist returned to check the statistics himself, and the magazine had already gone to press.
cn:
一位杂志主编曾计划刊登一篇关于某国总统新宫殿的文章。
为了掌握事实，他派了一名年轻记者去那个遥远的国家。
记者想把一切都写下来，于是写了一百多页。
主编读完文章后发现，作者偏偏漏掉了一个小小的数字。
他发去一份加急传真，询问宫殿到底花了多少钱。
不耐烦的主编等了好几天，却没有任何回音。
最后，记者亲自回去核对统计数字，而杂志早已付印了。

en:
One morning, the assistants at a jewellery shop were arranging necklaces on velvet trays.
While they were working, a large car stopped outside with its engine running.
Suddenly two men jumped out, smashed the shop window and grabbed everything within reach.
With its headlights on and its horn blaring, the car then raced down the arcade.
The shopkeeper ran out and threw whatever he could find at the raiders.
The thieves dropped some rings, and passers-by scrambled to pick them up.
The whole raid lasted less than a minute, and the car had disappeared before the police arrived.
cn:
一天早上，一家珠宝店的店员们正把项链摆上天鹅绒托盘。
他们正忙着的时候，一辆大轿车停在店外，发动机还开着。
突然两个人跳下车，砸碎橱窗，把够得着的东西抢掠一空。
随后，那辆车前灯亮着、喇叭长鸣，沿着拱廊街飞驰而去。
店主冲出来，抓起手边的东西就朝劫匪扔去。
劫匪掉落了几枚戒指，路人纷纷争抢拾捡。
整场抢劫不到一分钟，警察赶到之前汽车早已无影无踪。

en:
Have you ever left a banknote in a pocket and then washed it to pieces?
Damaged money has always been a headache, but banks are kinder than you might think.
Last month, our neighbour's puppy chewed up the savings that had been hidden in a drawer.
The mutilated notes were collected carefully and sent to the bank with a claim form.
Experts examined the remains and proved that the notes had really existed.
Because enough of each note was left, all the money has now been replaced.
Since then, the family has kept its cash far away from the dog.
cn:
你有没有把钞票忘在衣兜里、结果洗成碎片的经历？
残损的钞票向来令人头疼，但银行比你想象的要宽厚。
上个月，邻居家的小狗把藏在抽屉里的积蓄咬了个粉碎。
残缺的钞票被小心收集起来，连同索赔单一起寄给了银行。
专家们检验了残片，证明这些钞票确实存在过。
因为每张钞票都残留了足够的部分，如今所有的钱都已获兑换。
从那以后，这家人把现金放得离狗远远的。

en:
High in the mountains, an old monastery stands beside a famous pass.
It was founded about a thousand years ago as a shelter for travellers.
The monks living there have welcomed visitors for centuries, asking nothing in return.
Trained by the monks, the big dogs of the monastery once rescued many people from the snow.
Today a road has been built through the mountains, and the crossing is no longer dangerous.
Cars arrive all through the summer, and the monks' privacy is often disturbed.
So they prefer the winter, when deep snow keeps the crowds away and the mountains grow quiet again.
cn:
在高山之巅，一座古老的修道院矗立在著名的山口旁。
它创建于约一千年前，是为旅人提供庇护的住处。
住在那里的修士们数百年来一直接待来客，不求任何回报。
修道院的大狗经过修士训练，曾从雪地里救出许多人。
如今一条公路已穿山而过，翻越山口不再危险。
整个夏天汽车络绎不绝，修士们的清静常常被打扰。
所以他们更喜欢冬天——大雪挡住人群，群山重归宁静。

en:
Cats never fail to fascinate human beings, yet they accept our affection only when they choose.
Scientists are especially interested in the fact that cats usually land on their feet.
When a cat falls, instinct makes it turn its body quickly in the air.
It then spreads out its legs like a parachute to slow the fall.
Records show the surprising fact that cats falling from greater heights often suffer fewer injuries.
One cat is even said to have dropped thirty-two floors onto hard ground and survived.
Gravity, it seems, is one law that cats love to defy.
cn:
猫总能令人类着迷，可它们只在自己愿意时才接受我们的宠爱。
科学家尤其感兴趣的是：猫从高处落下时几乎总能四脚着地。
猫下落时，本能会让它在空中迅速转正身体。
然后它像撑开降落伞一样伸展四肢，减缓下坠的速度。
记录显示了一个惊人的事实：从更高处坠落的猫，受的伤往往反而更轻。
据说甚至有一只猫从三十二层楼摔到硬地上还活了下来。
看来，地心引力正是猫最爱挑战的一条法则。

en:
The great liner was described as the safest ship in the world before her first voyage.
Her builders believed that even if two of her watertight compartments were flooded, she would still float.
Rich passengers filled her luxurious rooms, and nobody thought about danger.
Four days after she had left port, a huge iceberg appeared in the icy darkness.
The ship turned sharply, but the ice had already torn a long hole in her side.
Five compartments were flooded, one more than she could bear, and the great ship trembled.
If there had been enough lifeboats, many more lives would have been saved.
The tragic voyage ended at the bottom of the Atlantic, and the world has never forgotten it.
cn:
这艘巨型班轮在处女航之前被誉为世界上最安全的船。
它的建造者相信，即使两个密封舱进水，船也依然能漂浮。
富有的乘客住满了豪华客舱，没有人想到危险。
离港四天之后，一座巨大的冰山出现在冰冷的黑暗中。
巨轮猛地转向，但冰棱已在船身划开了一道长长的口子。
五个船舱进了水——比它所能承受的多了一个，巨轮开始震颤。
如果当时有足够的救生艇，本可以多救下许多生命。
这次悲惨的航行终结在大西洋海底，而世人从未忘记它。

en:
When our ship docked early in the morning, I joined the long queue in the customs hall.
I had only clothes and books, yet I felt strangely guilty as the officer watched me.
He asked me whether I had anything to declare, and I told him that I had nothing.
Even so, he opened my case and examined every corner of it.
Suddenly he pounced on a small green bottle and smiled in a sarcastic way.
He said that perfume was not free of duty and that I should have declared it.
I explained that it was only hair gel, and his face turned red when he opened the bottle.
In the end, the tolerant officer drew a line on my case with chalk and waved me through.
cn:
清晨轮船靠岸后，我在海关大厅排进了长长的队伍。
我带的只有衣服和书，可在官员的注视下，我却莫名觉得心虚。
他问我有没有东西要申报，我告诉他什么都没有。
即便如此，他还是打开我的箱子，把每个角落都检查了一遍。
突然，他一把抓住一个绿色小瓶，露出讥讽的笑容。
他说香水可不免税，我本应该申报的。
我解释说那只是发胶；他拧开瓶子后，脸一下子红了。
最后，这位宽容的官员用粉笔在我的箱子上画了道线，挥手放行。

en:
Most of us have formed a very unrealistic picture of life on a desert island.
In our dreams, such an island is a paradise where the sun always shines.
If we actually lived there, we would soon discover the other side of the picture.
There is an element of truth in both views, but few of us have ever tested them.
Last year, two sailors were wrecked on a tiny island with a quiet lagoon.
They did not starve, for they caught fish in the lagoon and collected coconuts and rainwater.
They even built a rough raft, though the sea was far too wild for it.
When a ship finally rescued them, both men said that they would gladly return—for a holiday, not for survival.
cn:
我们大多数人对荒岛生活的想象都很不真实。
在梦里，这样的岛屿是阳光永驻的天堂。
可如果真的住在那里，我们很快就会发现事情的另一面。
两种看法都有几分道理，但我们中很少有人真正验证过。
去年，两名水手的船失事，漂到一座有平静环礁湖的小岛上。
他们没有挨饿，因为他们在环礁湖里捕鱼，还收集椰子和雨水。
他们甚至造了一只简陋的木筏，尽管大海对它来说太过狂暴。
最终获救时，两人都说愿意再回那座岛——不过是去度假，而不是求生。
